title: MPSC Gazetted Group B & C Combined Pre 2023 (English)

questions.text:
During the establishment of Labour Organisation 'Bombay Mill Hand Association' which of the following personalities supported Narayan Meghaji Lokhande?
a. Keshavrao Bagde, Keshavrao Bole
b. Raghu Bhikaji, Genu Babaji
c. Narayan Surkaji, Vitthalrao Korgaonkar
d. Krishnaji Arjun Keluskar, Ramchandra Shinde, Narayan Pawar
In 1857 uprising _______ Governor General said that, had Shinde and Nijam participated, then uprising would have been successful.

Which of the following statements are true about Prarthana Samaj?
a. Prarthana Samaj was established by Dadoba Tarkhadkar, Dr. Atmaram Pandurang, Vamani Abaji Modak, Bhau Mahajan, etc. on 31 March 1867 at Bombay.
b. Prarthana Samaj started 'Subodh Patrika'.
c. Through Prarthana Samaj N.M. Joshi established Social Service League to improve the conditions of labourers.
d. Prarthana Samaj established colleges at Mumbai, Solapur, Kolhapur.

Which of the following statements are true about 'Hindu Mahasabha'?
a. On 9 April 1915 Maharaja of Kasim Bazar, Sir Mahindra Chand Nandi announced the formation of Hindu Mahasabha at Haridwar.
b. B.S. Munje was the President of the Hindu Mahasabha.
c. Dr. Munje, Dr. Hedgewar, Dr. Paranjape started a branch of Hindu Mahasabha in Nagpur on 11 November 1923.
d. 28, 29, 30 December 1938 the Grand Session of the Hindu Mahasabha was held at Pune.

options.D: All of the above are correct

questions.text:
During the uprising of 1857 which British Superior Military Officers played an important role?
a. Neill, Havelock
b. Outram, Lawrence Brothers
c. Campbell, Sir Hume Rose
d. William Digby, Colin Clark

In which area did the peasant revolts of 1875 take place?
a. Bhimadi, Shirur, Parner, Nagar
b. Indapur, Haveli, Baglan, Satara
c. Tasgaon, Shirala, Jath, Malkapur
d. Shrigonda, Karjat, Solapur, Ratnagiri

In which of the following movements/agitations did Revolutionary Senapati Bapat participate?
a. Goa Liberation Movement
b. Hyderabad Liberation Movement
c. Samyukta Maharashtra Movement
d. Maharashtra – Mysore Border Agitation

options.D: All of the above are correct

questions.text:
In 1919 the British Government started the Constitutional Reforms because
a. Increased dissatisfaction among Indians due to the revolutionary movement
b. Alliance between Congress and Muslim League
c. Alliance between Extremist and Moderates
d. The political situation created by the First World War

options.D: All of the above are correct

questions.text: _______ attended the Delhi Durbar in 1877, dressed in Khadi for 'Shri Ganesha' of the Swadeshi Movement.

Sessions of the National Congress – Place → President
a. 1889 – Fifth Session – Bombay → I. Anandacharlu
b. 1891 – Seventh Session – Nagpur → II. Shankaran Nair
c. 1895 – Eleventh Session – Pune → III. William Wedderburn
d. 1897 – Thirteenth Session – Amravati → IV. Surendranath Banerjee

State → Population Density per sq. k.m. (2011)

b. Himachal Pradesh → ii. 414
c. Jharkhand → iii. 573
d. Bihar → iv. 1106

Peak, Place → Height (meter)
a. Mangi-Tungi → i. 1567
b. Trambakeshwar → ii. 1416
c. Saptashringi → iii. 1304
d. Salher → iv. 1100

State → Tourist Centre
a. Jammu and Kashmir → i. Taran Taran, Baba Bakala
b. Himachal Pradesh → ii. Ambala, Bhadkal lake
c. Punjab → iii. Avantipur, Alachigompa

Thermal Power Station → District
a. Koradi → i. Thane
b. Paras → ii. Nagpur
c. Uran → iii. Jalgaon
d. Fekari → iv. Akola

questions.text: Railseema Plateau is located in _______ region.

options.A: ₹816 and ₹1,000 per capita income per month in rural areas and urban areas respectively
options.B: ₹47 and ₹32 per capita income per day in rural areas and urban areas respectively
options.C: ₹49.63 and ₹56.64 per capita income per day in rural areas and urban areas respectively

a. Poverty eradication and employment growth
b. To develop hilly and tribal area

c. Taxation and budget
d. Parliament and administration

a. UTGST, is collected by Central Government and Union Territory Governments.
b. IGST, is collected by Central Government and State Governments.

options.C: Both a and b are incorrect

Who was the Marathi Literaturer to win the Gyanpeeth Award in 2021-22?

Which of the following statement is not correct about the Advocate General?
(1) The 'Advocate General' is appointed by the President of India
(2) He gives advice to the Government of the State on such legal matters which are referred to him by the Governor
(3) He perform such other duties of a legal character that is assigned to him by the Governor
(4) He discharges the functions conferred on him by the Constitution or any other law
Which among the following statement is not correct about a High Court in India?

options.A: High Courts may accept a petition related to 'Public Interest Litigation'
options.B: Every Judge of a High Court is appointed by the President
options.C: Governor of the State appoints the Judges of a High Court
options.D: The age of retirement of a High Court Judge is 62

questions.text: Which of the following day is celebrated by the Government of India every year on 24th April?

a. Bihar
b. Karnataka
c. Kerala
d. Rajasthan

Match the pairs correctly.
A. 61st Constitutional Amendment → 1. Retirement age of Members of State Public Service Commission increased from 60 to 62 years
B. 44th Constitutional Amendment → 2. Three tier Panchayat Raj System
C. 41st Constitutional Amendment → 3. Voter age changed from 21 to 18 years
D. 73rd Constitutional Amendment → 4. Controversy in Supreme Court and High Court regarding election of President, Vice President and Prime Minister

Consider the following statements about the powers and duties of Sarpanch.
a. To implement the resolutions passed in the Gram Sabha.
b. Protect the property owned by Gram Panchayat.
c. To supervise primary schools in the Gram Panchayat area.
Select the incorrect statements given above.

Which article was amended to clarify duties of the Election Commission?

I. Thomas Hobbes → A. Anglo-Austrian Philosopher
II. Friedric Hayek → B. Father of French Revolution
III. Robert Nozick → C. British Philosopher
IV. Rousseau → D. American Philosopher
Who among the following headed the Committee on the functions of the Constituent Assembly of India?

a. Protection and improvement of environment.
b. Improving living status and public health.
c. Protection of economic interests of SC, ST and weaker section.
d. To implement uniform civil code for all the people.

a. Prohibition of discrimination – Article 15
b. Right to Association – Article 19
c. Right to Protection of Life – Article 20
d. Right to Constitutional Remedies – Article 32

a. Contracts
b. Forests
c. Tolls
d. Bankruptcy and insolvency

options.A: Water neutralizes the pH of the soil
options.B: The roots are deprived of oxygen
options.C: Water supports the growth of root parasites
options.D: Water does not have all the necessary mineral for plant growth

questions.text: Whooping cough disease is caused by which of the following bacterial infection?
Which of the following vitamin acts as an antioxidant?
_______ are drugs which bind to receptors present in central nervous system and gastro-intestinal-tract.
German Silver is alloy of
Chemical name of aspirin is
The terms Hard and Soft – Acids and Bases were given by
If area of circle is 'x' sq.cm and perimeter of corresponding circle is 'x' cm, then which of the following will be radius of circle?
A shopkeeper sold 15 shirts for Rs. 2,340. He suffered a loss of Rs. 60. What is cost price of each shirt?
The sum of present ages of 3 persons is 72 yrs. Seven years ago, their ages were in the ratio 4 : 6 : 7. What are their present ages in years?
A man took a loan from a bank at the rate of 12% p.a. simple interest. After 3 years, he had to pay Rs. 5,400 as interest. Find the amount borrowed by him.
The value of machine decreases at the rate of 5% every year. If the value of machine is Rs. 16,000, what will it be after two years?
The railway fare increased by 20%. Again in next two successive months, it is increased by 10% and 25% respectively. What is the percentage increase in the original fare?
A batsman makes a score of 98 runs in the 19th innings and thus increases his average by 4. What is his average after 19th innings?
Find the radius of circle whose area is equal to the sum of areas of three circles of radii 3 cm, 4 cm and 12 cm.
How many squares are there on a chess board?

Find out the wrong term in the given series.
15, 21, 24, 30, 32, 39, 51

Two statements and two conclusions are given below. Find out suitable alternative.
Statements: A. All teachers are artists. B. Few artists live happily.
Conclusions: C. All teachers live happily. D. All those who live happily are artists.

options.C: Both C and D are false
options.D: Both C and D are true

questions.text: Ankita's mother's age is two and half times Ankita's age and four times her brother's age. Their total age is 66 years. What will be Ankita's brother's age after 2 years?

A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting on a wall and all of them are facing east.
i. C is the immediate right of D.
ii. B is at extreme end and E as his neighbour.
iii. G is between E and F.
iv. D is sitting third from the south end.
Who is sitting to the right of 'G'?
The International Yoga Day falls on Tuesday in 2016, then on which day will the National Science Day fall in the same year?
If the series 4, 5, 8, 13, 14, 17, 22,..., is continued in same pattern, which of the following is not a term of this series?
If all the letters of the word "UNIDENTIFIED" are arranged in alphabetical order, then the position of how many letters will remain unchanged?
Sanvi walks some distance towards East and turns left in 90°. Then she walks 50 m. Now she turns right in 135° and walks 15 m. Now her left hand indicates which direction?
Below Venn-diagram and three words are given in options. Which option represents the Venn-diagram correctly? (Three overlapping circles)
